title: Resume Architecture, Evidence Statements, and Machine-Robust Delivery · Hiring Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for resume architecture, evidence statements, and machine-robust delivery and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for truthful role-targeted resume system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.
Write concise action, context, decision, behavior, result, and evidence statements without false causality or invented metrics.
Translate projects, education, open source, community work, care, career change, and employment into relevant evidence without hiding provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resume Priority
steps.instruction:
Order the Resume Priority evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resume Priority.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Resume Priority assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resume Priority, keep Resume Priority connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The quiz variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Resume Priority has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resume Priority, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Resume Priority precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resume Priority, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The quiz variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resume Priority, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A resume must preserve the full chronology with equal detail.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Resume Priority case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the quiz Resume Priority case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the quiz Resume Priority case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the quiz Resume Priority case
